verses.title: Precious human life
verses.rootText: At this time when the difficult-to-gain ship of leisure and fortune has been obtained, ceaselessly hearing, pondering and meditating day and night in order to liberate others and oneself from the ocean of cyclic existence is the bodhisattvas' practice.

parts.title: The 37 Practices of the Bodhisattvas [1]
parts.content:
The 37 Practices of the Bodhisattvas [1]
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5NMcNAq8oFg
Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLaAW1H5vg2nHHpesQGHhUyu-l1Xa56nhf

Now the 37 practice of the bodhic sattva sam offers practical advice for those who really want to practice bodhicitta u in their daily life and want to take the bodhic sattva journey bodhic sattva path. So uh we have a good chance to understand how to practice bodhicitta based on uh this teaching 37 practice. Um so this text has as I said 37 verses and uh each describes a specific practice uh essential for the bodhicatta path. Um so please um um write down something that you really want to practice and put into your practice in daily. Uh you have to write down otherwise you are going to forget. Um so we're going to talk and study all the 37 verses step by step during this retreat. Um uh this is a kind of short text but it covers profound topics. Um and it um contains the essential points um and it um summarize the meaning of um great teachings as the bodhicara avatara. uh it also contains all the topics from the mind train and teachings and all of these are practice of bodhic satza. So we're going through all these uh 37 verses. Uh but um it might be um helpful if I first to give you uh the summary of this teaching. Um that's all my uh always my teachers when they start this teaching they give the summary so that you can keep um in your mind. So it has um three main principles. Uh first cultivating the motivation to follow the bodhicatta path. Second uh engaging in the specific trainings of the bodhic sattva path and third uh integrating those practice into your life. So first few verses emphasize um renouncing worldly attachments. They um encourage practitioners to reflect on the nature of life such as uh you know suffering of samsara, benefits of uh seeking liberation for the sake of all sentient beings. And then the following verses describe the specific practices uh bodhic sattva should cultivate. They explain how to overcome negative emotions and how to use difficult situation including harm caused by others as an opportunities to cultivate the six perfections paramas. Um and then the final verses focus on how to follow the bodhicatta path like uh practicing the union of bodhicitta and emptiness which is the uh heart essence of the bodhicattva journey. So each verse serve as a contemplation to help us uh face our lives more uh how do I say skillfully as well as an instruction from um the mind the instructions for uh meditation. So this teaching is a very um I would say practical guides for anyone who wish to uh develop both uh you know uh relative and ultimate bodhicitta. So I feel extremely fortunate to have received this teaching from both Ken and Kiuno not just once but many many times I memorized this text when I was 11 years old. Uh one time Kiapuno gave us this teaching and said that it is very profound and some of these verses may be difficult to practice right now but uh in he you know encouraged us uh not to lose your motivation or your strength because eventually we will be able to put them into practice. And he also said that we should not consider the dharma as simply trying uh to um receive blessings. Um did not like that. just you uh don't practice but just say prayers that's um not real or genuine dharma practitioners do. So we need to make an effort to practice what we have learned otherwise everything um intellectual. So dharma teachings is something we must uh personally put into action as much as possible and use this great opportunity. So this text summarize the entire bodhic sattva path and offers excellent um py instructions for uh mahayana practice. um it is not only popular in all schools of Tibetan Buddhism but also studied and practiced by Buddhists from many many different uh parts of the world. So this teaching is one of the uh puns favorite teachings and uh um he you know considered most important teaching and Kimu Panzo frequently taught this text at um Langar uh his monastery and also when he visited monasteries different place in Tibet, he would sometimes give the teachings in great detail um other times more sort of concisely and sometimes he given only the oral transmissions he always whenever he um you know visited in Tibet different monasteries he always uh focus on these teachings if he doesn't have time then he gave transmission that or reading transmission um so that's the that's a short introduction of this teaching. Okay. Now you have the text, right? You should uh look at the um the first the text start with a Sanskrit which is right. Um it is another name uh for aalog. Um so what is the reason why he um to um pays homage to a load at the beginning of uh this text. It is because a loadara uh is the uh physical manifestation of compassion of all bodhic sattvas and uh um among the qualities of Buddha the main quality is compassion. Right? So uh [Music] um so uh compassion um is the manifestation of the compassion right so compassion is the most important qualities of Buddha or bodhic sattva Buddha said, you know, whenever there is there is a space, there are sentient beings, right? Wherever sentient beings are, they are negative emotions and unhappiness. Therefore, if we wish for others, including ourselves, to overcome suffering, then compassion is important. And um in the madame Shandanda says compassion is the most important seed of uh enlightenment Buddhahood in the beginning, middle and the end. So there are u three main qualities uh that are possessed by the Buddhas. wisdom, compassion and power, right? Wisdom, compassion and power. That's the uh qualities Buddhas, you know. for the bodhicattva aspirating to the wealth of virtue. Every harm is like a precious tasure. Thus without resentment toward any being cultivating patience is the practice of bodhic sappa. Uh this verse expresses a key principle of patience. Sanskrit uh patience paramea which is one of the six parameas in Mahayana Buddhism. So again body suffers want to develop uh this paramea patience to benefit themselves and all other beings. For bodhic sattas actively involved and practicing um each and every time. Sometime something um goes wrong. It becomes an opportunity for the cultivation of patience rather than viewing an obstacle as a basis for resentment. So important right here it says every harm is like precious tasia not all bodhicat not me for bodhic sattas difficulties are not seen as obstacles but as opportunity to cultivate patience every harm is like precious tasure You see um the natural human response to harm is anger >> or resentment, right? But bodhic surface um seek to transcend this and for them it's like a precious tasure. So if we wish to achieve benefiting ourselves and others, it is absolutely essential that uh we cultivate patience because patience allows us to remain stable and check in responding with uh understanding rather than hatred. Instead of blaming others, bodhic sattas see the deeper causes of suffering. So practice and patience um and the in the face of harm is the transformation of suffering into spiritual growth. So this practice not only benefit ourselves by reducing suffering but also benefit others by bringing peace. So to practice patience, you first have to to start practicing with yourself and then for example, with your parents and then with your friends and so on. The first thing you have to do is I think not to speak but remain quiet, not arguing back and not reacting sort of negatively. Teachers say this is what you you have to do when you get upset. For example, someone hurts you and because of that if you get upset in this way you kind of injure your heart of bodhicitta and as a result the heart of bodhicitta is damaged. So your goal is until you achieve liberation, you must keep your heart of bodhicitta with you at all times, no matter what situation you are in. So you have to treat all um enemies remember which is the anger that harms you as your treasure then your bodhic chhatta will grow stronger and stronger. So, but usually we we don't really know how strongly we practice the dharma. Sometimes we feel we are okay but actually we're not because when someone hurts us we get upset there's no patience. Okay. 34th verse it says harsh words disturb the minds of others cause deterioration in a bodhic surface conduct. Thus giving of harsh words which are unpleasant to others is the practice of bodhic sattas. This verse is very important to practice, right? It deals with the importance of skillful speech and the practice of bodhic sattvas. It says harsh words disturb the minds of others. this there are several different ways we can speak harshly. We can directly say something harmful, hurtful to someone or another way is to say something behind someone's back, right? That will get back to them um and make them very very unhappy. So harsh words whether speaking out of anger, frustration or carelessness can deeply disturb others mind and harm relationships. Um they are unpleasant to everyone and this problem is very common. So how do we avoid harsh words? Avoiding harsh words requires mindfulness uh in Mahayana Buddhism and also a deep understanding of the impact your speech has on others. Observe your words and the intention behind them. [Music] Sometimes, as I said, right, sometimes we say something, there's not necessarily bad intention, motivation behind, but just we're not mindful. then we say something that's also hurts others. So mindfulness observe um my teacher say you know pause before speaking and ask yourself will these words bring benefit or not bring benefit or harm. Consider how the other person will feel upon hearing your words. Imagine yourself in their place. How would you like to be spoken to? This kind of like exchanging, right? Try to speak with the intention of helping rather than hurting. Harsh words arise from anger, irritation. So learn to recognize and manage these emotions before they manifest uh in speech. practice. Remember as I said practice um silence reflection or step away from situation if needed. Um avoid divisive speech. Don't create conflict or encourage misunderstanding. These all are my teachers say all the time. Remember that words cannot be taken back once spoken. So when feeling angry or frustrated, my teacher always say remain try to remain silent for a moment at least if you can. By integrating this practice into daily life, we can transform our speech into love and kindness. and bodhicitta and to make uh good connections rather than harm. We can think I am a Mahayana Buddhist practitioner. So I must at all times conduct myself um uh kindness, compassionate and subdue my mind and always act in a manner that is uh beneficial and not harmful towards others. If we go against this commitment of not doing or saying things um deliberately to hood others then our practice will diminish. That's why it says cause deterioration in a bodhic sattva's conduct. this verse say right. So for this reason we should give up harsh words that are not pleasing to others especially when someone is in a in a in a um leadership position uh it is very easy to become arrogant. um you know they think you know I can say whatever I want because I'm the person in in power um they believe in me and will follow me no matter what that is irrogant rightly thinking and acting like this uh diminish um our love compassion um and leads to harming others through harsh words, harsh words. So then does that mean that when you are in a leadership position for example you never say things that are unpleasant to other people? No at all. Not at all. Um avoiding harsh words does not mean avoiding difficult conversations. A bodhic sattva or any good leader may sometimes need to say things that are unpleasant for others to hear. But the key is um to do so skillfully um with the right motivation. Um so from your own side you don't have the motivation to hurt others when you speak with a good motivation. Does it mean that you will always be successful in not saying things are unpleasant to others? No. Sometimes people tell us you know things out of kindness but um our ego uh doesn't like to hear those things so we get upset and angry. You may not have any intention to harm when speaking with others. But if you are not mindful, your words may be unpleasant and cause you know cause unintentional harm. So some people are unaware of their um their um habitual arrogance. So their way of speaking comes across as arrogant uh even though they don't recognize it and this can harm others. This is why Buddha always say right mindfulness so important in speech. If you are practicing as a bodhic sattva it doesn't necessarily mean that all you say is pleasant words but all you say should be guided by wisdom and compassion. even if it is difficult for others to hear. So for example, if you are um leader of your there there are many different ways to speak, right? If you are leader of your workers, for example, you need to tell them that um their work is not enough. You don't have to say your work is terrible, right? Instead you can say like oh I noticed some areas where improvement is needed. So let's go over them together. That is good way to tell them, right? You don't have to say, "Oh, you're terrible. You're your work is not enough." That's what we call skillfully speak. If you are a teacher, you need to correct a student mistakes. It's the same thing. You don't have to say, "Oh, you are a bad student." You know, so being a bodhic satta or good leader does not mean avoiding difficult situations, but it means, you know, express them, you know, in a way that helps uh rather than harm. [Music] In our normal life, sometimes harsh words come from reacting too quickly. If we feel upset as I said my teacher say you know if you feel upset step back from the conversation before responding. So I think this verse is important to practice and remember all the time. Now here we have completed the explanation of the different practice of bodhic sattvas uh which consisted of the six paramatas. All right. Now we have here 35th verse which uh discusses how to abandon disturbing emotions. It says when it says one when emotions become habitual they are difficult to get rid of with antidotes. Thus with mindfulness and awareness eliminating them the moment they arise is the practice of bodhic sattas. Here we begin the bodhic satta practice of um eliminating uh disturbing emotions by explaining why they are so difficult to work with. If we let disturbing emotions um cla in Sanskrit right emotion um disturbing emotions become habituated. Then of course they um become stronger and stronger and stronger and stronger and at a certain points disturbing emotions become so strong then even if you apply their um antidote solution it's very very hard to overcome. Therefore, the key to overcome negative emotions is uh uh is uh catching them early. So we should not allow ourselves to get too used to them. A bodhic sattva does not wait for disturbing emotions to grow stronger. Instead they recognize them and let them dissolve at the very moment they arise. Then it easy to abandon them. That is the practice of bodhic satta. It says if you hold a fire in your hand will burn your hand. But if you throw away right away, you can save you can protect your head. parts.content:
37 Bodhisattva Practices Teachings - 2 - Winter ~ 2020/01/03 ~ H.E.Garchen Rinpoche
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=uYR3nmYFkN0
Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLcvNsUJcv64gOg5Yb-g0NH3-QTmV7Gxev

[Music] so good morning so for our opening prayers this morning we'll do the 37 practices of bodhisattvas okay is that better okay page number one I'm sorry starting on page number two in English no more Lucas water yay well seeing that all phenomena neither come nor go you strive solely for the sake of all beings supreme guru and protector Chenrezig at all times I respectfully pay homage to you with my body speech and mind the perfect Buddha is the source of all benefit and happiness the rise from having accomplished the true Dharma since that depends on knowing the practices I shall explain the way of the Bodhisattvas having now gained this great ship of freedom and fortune so difficult to find in order to free yourself and others from the ocean of cyclic existence listen reflect and meditate day and night without distraction this is the way of a bodhisattva attachment to loved ones turns like water hatred for enemies rages like fire and the darkness of ignorance one forgets what to adopt and what to avoid abandon your homeland this is the way of a bodhisattva when negative places are abandoned disturbing emotions will gradually decline without distractions attention to virtue naturally grows when the mind becomes clear certainty in the Dharma arises live alone in seclusion this is the way of a bodhisattva family and longtime companions will part from each other possessions gained with effort will be left behind consciousness a guest will leave the guest house of the body it concerns this is the way of a bodhisattva a bad company the three poisons grows stronger and the activities of learning reflecting and meditating decline and love and compassion are lost give up such companions this is the way of a bodhisattva when one follows a true spiritual friend false will dissolve and good qualities will grow like the waxing consider this friend more dear than your own body this is the way of a bodhisattva who can worldly gods themselves imprisoned in samsara protect therefore seek refuge in the three jewels the undeceiving source of refuge this is the way of a bodhisattva the Buddha taught that all the unbearable suffering of the lower realms is caused by wrongdoing therefore never act wrongly even at the cost of your life this is the way of a bodhisattva like a dewdrop on a blade of grass the happiness of the three worlds vanishes in but an instant strive for the supreme state of never changing liberation this is the way of a bodhisattva when mothers who have loved you since beginningless time are suffering what good is your own happiness therefore give rise to bodhichitta in order to liberate infinite sentient beings this is the way sattva all suffering without exception comes from seeking your own happiness the perfect Buddha's are born from the altruistic mind therefore truely exchange your own happiness for the suffering of others this is the way of a bodhisattva even if someone driven by great desire steals all your wealth or lets others steal it dedicate to them your body possessions in all virtues of the three times this is the way of a bodhisattva even if someone cuts off your head when you haven't done anything wrong take all the negative deeds upon yourself through the power of compassion this is the way of a bodhisattva even if others slander you throughout a billion worlds return speak with a loving mind of their good qualities this is the way of a bodhisattva even if someone exposes your hidden faults and insults you in front of others view that person as a teacher and bow with respect this is the way of a bodhisattva even if someone you have cared for as dearly as your own child regards you as an enemy love that person even more as a mother would hurt a lame child this is the way of a bodhisattva even if someone of equal or lower status driven by pride treats you with disdain respectfully placed them on your crown as your teacher this is the way of a bodhisattva thogme though you may be impoverished and always disparage gripped by diseased and tormented by spirits never lose courage but take on the misdeeds and the pain of beings this is the way of a bodhisattva though you may be famous and revered by many and it was rich as the Well of God himself having seen that worldly fortune is without essence do not be proud this is the way of a bodhisattva if the enemy your own anger is not tamed instead of doing other enemies will only make them increase therefore subdue your own mind with the army of love and compassion this is the way of a bodhisattva sensory flexors are like salt water the more you drink the greater your first abandon applauds all things that give rise to cleaning attachments this is the way of a bodhisattva the way things appear is your own mind mind yourself primordial ii transcends the fabricated extremes knowing justice do not create concepts of subject or object this is the way of a bodhisattva when you come across things that attract you like beautiful rainbows the summer times do not regard them as real an abandoned clinging attachment this is the way of a bodhisattva all the suffering is like the death of your child in the dream how exhausting it is to hold illusory appearances as real therefore when you meet with difficult circumstances see them as this is the way everybody's thoughtful when even one's body must be given up when aspiring for enlightenment what need is there to mention material things there practice generosity without a hopeful reward or comic results this is the way of a bodhisattva if you lack moral discipline you cannot accomplish even your own purpose so wanting to achieve the purpose of others is truly absurd therefore protect your discipline without concern for worldly existence this is the [Music] good morning so we'll start with our opening prayers the little booklet 37 practice as the way of a bodhisattva please raise your hand if you need one of these and we'll begin on page two no more locust what I will seem that all phenomenon neither come nor go you strive solely for the sake of all beings supreme guru and protector Chenrezig at all times i respectfully pay homage to you with my body speech and mind the perfect buddha's source of all benefit and happiness arise from having accomplished the true dharma since that depends on knowing the practices I shall explain the way of Bodhisattvas having now gained this great ship of freedom and fortune so difficult to find in order to free yourself and others from the ocean cyclic existence listen reflect and meditate day and night without distraction this is the way of a bodhisattva attachment to loved ones turns like water hatred for enemies rages like fire in the darkness of ignorance one forgets what to adopt and want to avoid abandon your homeland this is the way of a bodhisattva when negative places are abandoned disturbing emotions will gradually decline without distractions attention to virtue naturally gross when the mind becomes clear certainty the Dharma arises live alone in seclusion this is the way of a bodhisattva family and longtime companions will part from each other possessions gained with effort will be left behind consciousness a guest will leave the guest house of the body let go of all worldly concerns this is the way of a bodhisattva in bad company the three poisons grow stronger the activities of learning reflecting and meditating decline and love and compassion are lost give up such companions this is the way of a bodhisattva when one follows a true spiritual friend faults will dissolve and good quality school grow like the waxing moon consider this friend more dear than your own body this is the way of a bodhisattva who can worldly gods themselves imprisoned in a samsara protect therefore seek refuge in the three jewels the undeceiving source of refuge this is the way of a bodhisattva the buddha taught that all the unbearable suffering of the lower realms who is caused by wrongdoing therefore never act wrongfully even at the cost of your life this is the way of a bodhisattva like a dewdrop on a blade of grass the happiness of the three worlds vanishes and but an instant strive for the supreme state of never changing liberation this is the way of a bodhisattva when mothers who have loved you since beginningless time are suffering what good is your own happiness and therefore give rise to bodhichitta in order to liberate infinite sentient beings this is the way of a bodhisattva all suffering without exception comes from seeking your own happiness the perfect buddhas are born from the altruistic mind therefore is truly exchange your own half for the suffering of others this is the way of a bodhisattva even if someone driven out of desire steals all your wealth or lets others steal it dedicate to them your body possessions in all virtues of the three times this is the way of a bodhisattva even if someone cuts off your head when you haven't done anything wrong take all their negative deeds upon yourself through the power of compassion this is the way of a bodhisattva even if others slander you throughout a billion worlds in return speak with a loving mind of their good qualities this is the way of a bodhisattva even if someone exposes your hidden faults and insults you in front of others view that person as a teacher and bow with respect this is the way of a bodhisattva even if someone you have cared for as dearly as your own child regards you as an enemy loved that person even more as a mother would her ailing child this is the way of a bodhisattva even if someone of equal or lower status driven by pride treats you with disdain respectfully placed them on your crown as your teacher this is the way of a bodhisattva though you may be impoverished always disparaged gripped by disease and tormented by spirits never lose courage but take on the misdeeds and the pain of all beings this is the way of a bodhisattva though you may be famous and revered by many and are as rich as the wealth god himself having seen that worldly fortune is without essence do not be proud this is the way of a bodhisattva if the enemy your own anger is not tamed subduing other enemies will only make them increase therefore subdue your own mind with the army of love and compassion this is the way of a bodhisattva sensory pleasures are like salt water the more you drink the greater your thirst abandon at once all things that give rise to clinging attachment this is the way of a bodhisattva the way things appear is your own mind mind itself primordial ii transcends fabricated extremes knowing just this do not create concepts of subject and object this is the way of a bodhisattva when you come across things that attract you like beautiful rainbows in summertime do not regard them as real and abandon clinging attachment this is the way of a bodhisattva all suffering is like the death of your child in a dream how exhausting it is to hold illusory appearances as real therefore when you meet with disagreeable circumstances see them as illusory this is the way of a bodhisattva when even once body must be given up when aspiring
